details.text: Derivative: زَلْزَلَةَ means shaking or earthquake. زَلَّ in Arabic means to slip. Its رُباعِي (quadruple) form is زَلْزَلَ which implies to continually slip in the case where the earth keeps shaking and the people keep on slipping and are unable to stand firm.
Derivative: حَرْف on the edge (of a cliff), verge or brink.
Derivative: الخُسْران is worse that خُسْر (loss) and is its مُبالَغَة form (exaggerated) translating as the ultimate or horrific loss.
- Literally: “He worships Allah upon the edge of a cliff” meaning that he is not steady in his worship as he is walking on the edge in his worship and could easily slip off.
- This literally means to turn around upon his face, which means that he turned around full circle and entirely.
Derivative: عاشَرَ- يُعاشِرُ means to live with family and so عَشِير can refer to a family member, companion or close person.
Derivative: اِخْتَصَمَ – اِخْتِصاماً means to argue or quarrel aggressively or be in conflict about something.
Derivative: صَبَّ – يَصُبُّ – صَبّاً means to pour a large amount with a gush and not a gentle or slow pouring.
Derivative: مُرْضِعَة refers to any mother who feeds her child or offspring with milk.
Derivative: تَذْهَلُ from ذَهَلَ means to forget something due to shock or to be stunned due to fear.
Derivative: إِلْحاد refers here to deviation from the established truth or tradition.

Derivative: العَاكِف refers to the one who lives in Makkah and moved their due to the greatness of the Ka’bah. It refers to someone who stays somewhere due to the religious greatness or specialness he feels the place has.
Derivative: صَدَّ – يَصُدُّ – صدّاً means to obstruct or block yourself and to obstruct or block others. That s because صَدَّ is both لازِم (intransitive) and مُتَعَدٍّ (transitive).
Derivative: البَاد relates to a place that is far from civilisation and does not have many buildings. البَاد refers to those who are Bedouins and come from outside of Makkah and visit it.
Derivative: بَوَّأَ – تَبْويع and تَبَوَّعَ – تَبَوُّع means to move and settle somewhere from one place to another and implies the meaning that this new place is ideal and fulfils all one’s needs.

- وَالرُّكَّعِ السُّجُودِ is stated without a وَ (واوُ العَطْف – meaning “and”) between them as the bowing and prostration are considered as one matter: The Salah (prayer). It means: Those who bow and prostrate i.e., those who perform the prayer.
Derivative: رِجَالًا means by foot from رِجْلٌ (a foot).
Derivative: ضَامِر means skinny or lean ride in the case where the skinniness or leanness is the result of the long journey.
- مِن كُلِّ فَجٍّ عَمِيقٍ literally means from every deep mountain and is used as an expression to mean every far and distant place.
Derivative: البائِس is the one who is in critical need or in a difficult situation due to hunger or war.
Derivative: العَتِيق means pure in origin or a place that is very old and ancient and over the years has not lost its sanctity or value.
- The phrase قَضَى تَفَثَهُ means the person cleaned himself up by removing dirt and rectifying his dishevelled state, by cutting nails and other such acts.
Derivative: مَرِيد is the rebel that is leading the way in rebelliousness.
Derivative: ذَٰلِكَ at the beginning of the verse and separated from what follows is a style that means: All that we have just mentioned i.e., take heed of it.

Derivative: أَوْثان is the plural of وَثَنٌ and refers to statues or rocks or trees or some water source that is considered sacred which have remained in their place for a long period of time. It differs from أَصْنام the plural of صَنَم which is a manufactured carved idol.
Derivative: حُرُمات are those matters which are not permitted to be violated and which must be upheld and respected with the utmost level of respect i.e., sanctities.
Derivative: زُور is falsehood like بَاطِل or an untruth.
Derivative: حُنَفاء is the plural of حَنيف and means entirely and solely focused and dedicated without any distraction.
Derivative: سَحِيقٍ means battered and pulverised or far away and distant i.e., a place that nobody will find.

Derivative: شَعائِر comes from شُعُور and مَشْعَر which means feeling, recognition and awareness. There are certain things or symbols that raise the awareness of Allah. Those places and matters which generate an awareness of Allah, a recognition of Allah and make you feel closer to Allah are شَعائِر. The شَعائِر can also be translated as the main features, signs or symbols of the Deen.
Derivative: المُخْبِتينَ those whose hearts have become soft. خِبَت refers to soft land due to rain. Allah’s describes the personality and heart that become soft like that and are humble.

- وَالْمُقِيمِي الصَّلَاةِ has الْ attached to مقيمي which is an exception in the language to highlight or give greater emphasis to something. Here, those who perform the Salah are highlighted. This structuring is also known as “shortening in the speech” (التَّكْسير في الكلام). In origin it is الذين هُمْ مُقيمو الصلاةِ and is shortened to وَالْمُقِيمِي الصَّلَاةِ.
Derivative: بُدْنٌ is the specific name for a camel that is taken to Hajj for the purpose of sacrifice.
Derivative: المُعْتَرّ the one who is needy and asks.
Derivative: القانِع is the one who does not ask and is content with what he is given.
- Literally means: “When its side is mandated” which means when the camel inevitably tips over and falls to the ground after the slaughtering.
Derivative: خَوَّان is the one who is extremely and repeatedly deceitful or treacherous.
Derivative: كَفُور is the one who is extremely ungrateful or extreme in his denial of the favours of Allah.
Derivative: صلَواتٌ refers to Jewish places of worship or synagogues.
Derivative: بِيَعٌ is the plural of بِيعَة and refers to Christian places of worship or churches.
Derivative: صَوامِع is the plural of صَوْمَعَة and means a monastery where devout worship is undertaken.
Derivative: أَمْلَيْتُ from أَمْلَى which means to provide with more time or a respite i.e., to continue their enjoyment or in their disbelief before the punishment comes to them.

Derivative: نَكِيرِ is from نَكِرَ something that is not well-known and نَكِيرِ is also used to mean something that is intolerable. Here it means: “How did these people find my censure and punishment?!”.

Derivative: مَّشِيدٍ can mean a building that has been reinforced by plaster in the case where شَادَ الحَائِط is used in Arabic to mean: He plastered the wall. مَشيد can also mean tall and lofty from شادَ which also means to build and construct.
Derivative: مُعطَّل means to be unused and unusable or out of service. It is something that has been rendered useless.
- It literally means that they were “turned over upon their rooftops” and is an expression to mean destruction and to be left in complete ruin.
Derivative: اِهْتَزَّ means to vibrate
Derivative: بَهِيج is a word used to describe beauty and something delightful.
Derivative: To be returned back to something or state.
Derivative: مُخَلَّقَة moulded, sculptured or shaped.

Derivative: أَرْذَلِ means below everything else. People would stay away from such a person characterised by this due to his despicability and contemptibility. Here it is linked to age (أَرْذَلِ الْعُمُرِ) and depicts the age where people become uncomfortable to be in their presence, the most deteriorated age in terms of decrepitude and senility.
Derivative: To settle and station calmly.
Derivative: عَلَقَة a hanging piece of flesh from عَلِقَ which means to be stick and cling.

- so that you can become strong bodied i.e., become an adult.
- بَلَغَ أَشُدَّهُ means to attain maturity.
- “A well-named time” or “An appointed time”.
Derivative: مُعَٰجِزِينَ refers to those who compete and make a lot of effort in vain to defeat or refute their opponent.

- “When he (A Messenger of Prophet) wishes (for something) the Shaytan throws or inserts something into their wish (i.e., to divert or distort it)”. Some interpreted this to mean that Shaytan succeeded in whispering to the Prophets or Messengers. However, an alternative understanding is that Shaytan works to impede the plans and wishes of the Prophets and Messengers through others and not by having any direct influence or effect upon them. In another words, Shaytan works to sabotage the wishes of the Prophets.
details.arabic_part: إِذَا تَمَنَّىٰٓ أَلْقَى ٱلشَّيْطَٰنُ فِىٓ أُمْنِيَّتِهِ

details.text: - “And We have not sent any رَّسُولٍ (Messenger) or نَبِىٍّ (Prophet)”. The structuring of the Ayah makes it evident that there is a difference between the رَّسُولٍ and the نَبِىٍّ because when they come together, then this is to highlight that there is a difference.
details.arabic_part: وَمَآ أَرْسَلْنَا مِن قَبْلِكَ مِن رَّسُولٍ وَلَا نَبِىٍّ

details.text: - This phrasal construct is النَّعْتُ السَّبَبِي (An-Na’t As-Sababiy) where the صِفَة (description) comes before the مَوْصوف (That which is being described) to describe here those “whose hearts have become hardened”.
Derivative: تُخْبِتَ from أَخْبَتَ means to soften and be melted when speaking about the hearts and to become humbled more generally.

Derivative: عَقِيم originally refers to a barren land that does not produce any vegetation, for a man of woman who cannot have children who are barren and to describe a wind devoid of any benefit. Here in respect to the punishment associated with the day of judgement, it means a day that has no good or relief in it.
Derivative: مِرْيَة means to have doubt about something that there should be no doubt concerning it.
Derivative: بَغْتَةً means without expectation, out of nowhere and suddenly.
Derivative: نَعِيم refers to permanent or perpetual blessings and luxury.
Derivative: حَلِيمٌ means to be forbearing and sympathetic.
Derivative: مُدْخَل means the place that they will be made to enter through referring to the entry to paradise.
Derivative: غَفُورٌ means “extremely” forgiving due to the use of the amplifying form فَعُول.
Derivative: الْغَنِيُّ means to be free of all needs, independent and self-sufficient.
Derivative: الْحَمِيدُ is the one who “perpetually” praised due to the use of the فَعِيل form which expresses perpetuity or continuity.
Derivative: رَحِيمٌ means the one who is “perpetually” merciful due to the use of the فَعِيل form which expresses perpetuity or continuity.
Derivative: رَءُوفٌ means “extremely” compassionate and empathetic due to the use of the amplifying form فَعُول.
Derivative: السَّماء in Arabic does not just mean the sky but also refers to everything that is above you.
Derivative: كَفُورٌ means “extremely” ungrateful due to the use of the amplifying form فَعُول.
Derivative: يُنازِعُ from نازَعَ. نَزْعٌ means to snatch or pull back something that is resting. نازَعَ means to yank or pull at one another which carries the meaning of disputing or arguing.
Derivative: تَعْمَلُونَ from عَمِلَ – يَعْمَلُ – عَمَلاً. عَمَلٌ differs from فِعْلٌ as it includes both action and intent.
Derivative: أَنَّ (Anna) indicates that it is connected to what had been stated previously whilst إِنَّ (Inna) indicates to the beginning of a new statement.
Derivative: يَسْطُونَ from سَطَا – يَسْطُو – سَطْوَة which means to grab forcefully or to attack and pounce upon.
Derivative: الْمُنْكَر denial, here based on hatred and aversion. الْمُنْكَرalso refers to evil. Here it refers to what you will see in the faces of the disbelievers in terms of denial and evil.
Derivative: سَلَبَ – يَسْلُبُ - سَلْباً means to snatch something away before your eyes

- ضُرِبَ مَثَلٌ means: “An example has been set forth or presented”. This has been stated in the Majhool form where the one who has presented the example is not mentioned. This places the emphasis on the example and not the one who has provided it.
- ضُرِبَ is from ضَرْب which means to strike. what is the significance of using This with مَثَلٌ (an example) where it is stated that “He struck (ضَرَبَ) an example”? it can be said that the examples are striking i.e., they catch the attention.

- The literal translation is: “The seeker or one making a demand (الطَّالِب) and that which is sought or being demanded (المَطْلُوب) are both weak”. It highlights the weakness of all parties involved; the idol that is being worshipped and the fly. They are all inherently weak. Everybody who wants something is weak and the thing they want is also weak.
Derivative: يَصْطَفِى – اِصْطَفَى means to choose with no biases or influences.

Derivative: تُفْلِحُونَ from أَفْلَحَ which means to attain success and its literal meaning is to reap the harvest. المُفْلِحُ refers to the one who has put a lot of effort into his farm and then saw the fruits of his labour in the harvest.
Derivative: حَرَج is a tightness of the chest to refer to difficulty.
Derivative: اِجٍتَبَى means to choose based on merit, qualities or qualification i.e., to undertake a role, mission or task etc.
Derivative: مِلَّة refers to a nation that is unified together by love, devotion and belief. It can also refer to the Deen and the Sharee’ah.

Derivative: النَّصِير – نُصْرَةٌ means help and aid against an enemy or difficult odds. Allah is the most excellent نصير (helper) who alone can be relied upon in the times of need to provide aid and assistance.
Derivative: جَٰهِدُوا۟ is from جاهَدَ and it means to exhaust yourself to your utmost level which differs from جَهَدَ which means to make effort.
- The difference between جَاهِدوا فِي اللهِ and جَاهِدوا فِي سَبِيلِ اللهِ: The removal of the سبيل (the way or path of) makes the attachment and connection more direct and closer to Allah.

- وَاعْتَصَمُوا بِاللَّهِ – “And hold fast to Allah, to have trust and reliance upon Him”. This is a more direct statement in terms of attachment and closeness to Allah than the statement of Allah: وَاعْتَصِمُوا بِحَبْلِ اللَّهِ “And hold fast to the rope of Allah) in Surah Aali ‘Imran verse 103 as حَبْل (rope) is omitted making the attachment and connection direct to Allah.
Derivative: مُنِير is something that gives off, produces or radiates light (نُور).
Derivative: خِزْىٌ is when your shame or disgrace is shown in front of other.
- ثَانىَ عِطْفِهِ literally means that he turned his neck or shoulders but as an expression it means to turn away in arrogance.
